prompt: Run spec-retro on sp003. The reports SMTP retry just shipped; im003 is the new implementation. Find and flag any feature-extraction candidates based on actual cross-im reuse.

assertions.text: Skill lists $AKM_ROOT/docs/notes/im*.md to cross-scan other implementations
Skill reads im001.md and im002.md (in addition to im003) to check for shared symbols/files
Skill identifies src/lib/retry.go (or 'RetryWithJitter' / 'retry-with-jitter') as a candidate symbol used by all three implementations
Skill produces a 'Candidate Features:' block in the final summary
The candidate explicitly names im001 AND im002 as consumers, not just a generic 'others would benefit' phrase

The candidate is presented as a recommendation for the user to verify — no new ft002.md or ft003.md file was created, no consuming im###.md was rewritten to reference a not-yet-minted feature, and the response asks the user to decide / confirm before any mint

Correlation strength is assessed explicitly — the response notes that the three im### use the same retry helper with the same shape (same signature, same call pattern), not just that they happen to touch the same file
The retro commits on main with message matching 'feat(akm): retro sp003' convention
im003.md was rewritten (mtime newer than seed); other im### files were NOT mutated (only read for cross-scan)
